kb.text: ReaderPub is a web-native publishing network that turns books and knowledge publications from static files into browser-readable assets that can be searched, discussed, shared and connected to readers.
kb.dom_path: body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[1] > details[1] > p
body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[1] > details[2] > summary

kb.text: Web-native publishing means a book or publication opens directly in the browser, has a web presence, and can stay connected to discovery, discussion, author presence and audience activity.
kb.dom_path: body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[1] > details[2] > p

kb.text: Is ReaderPub only an ebook store?
kb.dom_path: body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[1] > details[3] > summary

kb.text: No. ReaderPub includes publishing, reading, discussion and knowledge layers. Commerce is part of the model, but the larger goal is keeping publications active on the web.
kb.dom_path: body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[1] > details[3] > p

kb.text: What products are included in ReaderPub?
kb.dom_path: body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[1] > details[4] > summary

kb.text: ReaderPub includes WePub for publishing, WeRead for browser-based reading, WeBuzz for book discussion and discovery, and BookTree for structured notes, excerpts and research.
kb.dom_path: body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[1] > details[4] > p

kb.text: What makes ReaderPub different from traditional digital publishing?
kb.dom_path: body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[1] > details[5] > summary

kb.text: Traditional publishing often ends with file delivery. ReaderPub keeps books and publications readable, searchable, discussable and connected after release.
kb.dom_path: body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[1] > details[5] > p
body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[2] > h2

kb.text: How can I start reading on ReaderPub?
kb.dom_path: body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[2] > details[1] > summary

kb.text: Open the ReaderPub catalog, choose a book, and start reading directly in WeRead, ReaderPub's browser-based reading layer. No separate reading app is required.

kb.text: How large is the ReaderPub catalog?

kb.text: WeRead is ReaderPub's browser-native reader. It lets books open from a link and keeps reading connected to notes, discussion, discovery and community.

kb.text: Do I need to download books before reading?
kb.dom_path: body > div > main > section[3] > div > div[2] > details[4] > summary

kb.text: No. ReaderPub is designed for web reading. Books can open directly in the browser, so readers can start immediately without downloading a separate file.
